model.label: DeepSeek V3.2
model.description: DeepSeek • General-purpose V-series model optimized for reasoning, coding, and efficient inference.

pick.rationale: Virginia's slightly superior efficiency metrics and higher seeding give them a narrow edge in what projects as a defensive battle. Both teams feature elite top‑15 defenses, but the Cavaliers' more efficient offense and disciplined style should produce just enough scoring opportunities to advance. In a low‑possession game where every basket matters, Virginia's experience and consistency will be decisive.

reasoningStep.title: Efficiency and seeding comparison
reasoningStep.summary: Virginia holds slight edges in adjusted efficiency margin (26.49 vs 26.06 per KenPom) and is the higher seed (3 vs 6), while both teams feature elite defenses ranked 13th and 14th nationally.
evidence: Torvik ranks: Virginia 14th, Tennessee 15th. KenPom ranks: Virginia 13th, Tennessee 15th. Virginia's adjusted efficiency margin is 26.49 vs Tennessee's 26.06. Virginia's record is 29‑4, Tennessee's 22‑11. Both defenses rank in the top 14 nationally, with Tennessee's defense slightly better (13th vs 14th) and Virginia's offense slightly better (28th vs 35th in KenPom).

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
Bracket config id: 2026-bracket
Tournament year: 2026
Current game id: r32-midwest-3
Current game label: Midwest Round of 32 3
Round: Round of 32
Resolved teams: Tennessee (tennessee) vs Virginia (virginia)
Prior committed picks: 50

I need to predict the winner of the Midwest Round of 32 game between Tennessee and Virginia. First, I should gather information about both teams' ratings, recent form, injuries, and any relevant context. Let me start by getting their efficiency ratings.

Both teams have very similar ratings: Tennessee is ranked 15th in Torvik and 15th in KenPom, Virginia is 14th in Torvik and 13th in KenPom. Both have elite defenses: Tennessee's defense rank is 13th in Torvik, Virginia's is 14th. Offenses are slightly lower but still top-35. Virginia has a much better record (29-4 vs 22-11), but that could be due to strength of schedule. Tennessee's SEC schedule might be tougher than Virginia's ACC schedule? Need to examine further. Let me search for recent news, injuries, and matchup analysis.

modelTrace.content:
Search seems unavailable. I'll have to rely on ratings and logical reasoning. Also, note that in prior picks, Tennessee beat SMU in the first round, Virginia beat Wright State. Both were high confidence picks. Now they face each other.

I should consider playing styles: Virginia is known for slow tempo, pack-line defense. Tennessee under Rick Barnes is also defensive-minded. This could be a low-scoring grinder. Which team is better suited for that? Both are excellent defensively. Virginia's offense might be more efficient, but Tennessee's defense is slightly better. Could be a toss-up.

I need to decide a winner. Let me think about tournament experience: Both programs have recent tournament success. Virginia won a national championship in 2019, Tennessee has been to Sweet 16s. But that's historical; this is 2026. Coaches: Tony Bennett vs Rick Barnes. Both are experienced.

Perhaps I should look at the teams' performance against similar opponents? Not possible without data.

I could consider the "eye test": Virginia's record is much better, but maybe they had an easier schedule. The ACC might be stronger than SEC? In 2026, who knows? Typically ACC is strong, SEC also strong.

Let me check the prior picks for any clues about the bracket's assumptions. In the prior picks, Tennessee was picked over SMU with high confidence, Virginia over Wright State with high confidence. No indication of an upset.

I need to make a pick. I'll go with Virginia because they have a slightly higher efficiency ranking (13 vs 15) and a much better record, suggesting they might be more consistent. However, Tennessee's tougher schedule might explain the record difference. Actually, Tennessee's 22-11 record includes many losses against tough SEC opponents, while Virginia's 29-4 might include fewer tough games. But both are high seeds? Let me check seeds. Not provided in ratings. But they are likely 4 vs 5 seed or something. In the bracket, this is Midwest Round of 32 game 3, which could be a 4-5 matchup. I need to see the bracket structure. Let me get game definitions.

So Tennessee is a 6 seed (since they are in a 6/11 game), and Virginia is a 3 seed (3/14 game). That's important! Virginia is the higher seed. In my prior picks, I had Virginia as a 3 seed beating 14 seed Wright State, and Tennessee as 6 seed beating 11 seed SMU. Now 3 seed vs 6 seed. That suggests Virginia is favored. The efficiency ratings are close, but seed indicates Virginia is considered stronger by the selection committee. I should respect that, but also consider upset potential.

Given the close ratings, this could be a toss-up. I need to assign confidence. I'll go with Virginia, confidence Medium, because it's a close matchup but the higher seed and slightly better metrics give them an edge.

But let me also think about matchup specifics: Virginia's slow tempo vs Tennessee's defensive pressure. Virginia's offense is more efficient, Tennessee's defense is slightly better. Could be a defensive slugfest. Which team has more offensive creators? Unknown.

I'll search for any cached knowledge about 2026 teams? Not possible. I'll make a reasoned pick.

Let me also consider that Virginia's defense is elite, but Tennessee's offense is weaker. So Virginia might have a slight edge because they can score more efficiently against Tennessee's defense than Tennessee can against Virginia's defense.

Calculate adjusted efficiency margins: Torvik adjoe minus adjde. Virginia: 121.75 - 95.67 = 26.08. Tennessee: 121.51 - 95.48 = 26.03. Almost identical. Virginia slightly higher. KenPom adj EM: Virginia 26.49, Tennessee 26.06. So Virginia has a slight edge.

Thus, pick Virginia.

Rationale: Virginia's slightly superior efficiency metrics and higher seeding give them a narrow edge in a defensive battle. Their disciplined style and experience should prevail in a low-possession game.

Now I need to produce JSON with pick and reasoningStep. Let me structure reasoningStep with evidence from ratings.

I'll set reasoningStep id as "analysis-1", title "Efficiency comparison", summary "Virginia holds slight edges in adjusted efficiency margin and seeding, while both teams feature elite defenses."

Evidence: include the ratings numbers.

Now finalize.
